subject: Geography
chapter: The World Population Distribution, Density and Growth

questions.q: Which of the following countries has the highest population density?
Describe the demographic transition model. (5 marks)
questions.answer: The demographic transition model is a theory that explains how the population of a country changes from a high birth rate and death rate to a low birth rate and death rate.

questions.q: Explain the concept of population growth rate. (8 marks)
questions.answer: The population growth rate is the rate at which the population of a country is increasing. It is calculated by subtracting the death rate from the birth rate.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The population of India is approximately ______ million.
Which of the following is a characteristic of the first stage of demographic transition?
questions.answer: High birth rate and high death rate

questions.q: Describe the factors that influence population distribution. (5 marks)
questions.answer: The factors that influence population distribution are climate, soil, water, natural vegetation, and mineral resources.

questions.q: True or False: The population of China is decreasing.
Explain the concept of rural-urban migration. (8 marks)
questions.answer: Rural-urban migration is the movement of people from rural areas to urban areas in search of better employment opportunities.

questions.q: Which of the following countries has a high population growth rate?
Describe the concept of population pyramids. (5 marks)
questions.answer: A population pyramid is a graphical representation of the age and sex distribution of a population.
